sections.description: SCM and Site Activities/Areas Screens Explained

sheets.description: SCM and Site Activities/Areas Screens Explained

entries.label:
Dev Notes (Hidden): Both the EXISTING and NEW SCM sets of screens use the exact same set of fields and export labels, which allows them to share a single Canvas Download class (SWRoutineInspSCM) and post stored proc. Any field added to one screen must be added to the other (can be hidden with default), and appear in both the model and stored proc input parameters. 

The IsNewSCM field is used to flag new SCMs identified by users. This is only used to prompt deeper review in ARTEMIS. Otherwise NEW and EXISTING are treated the same when saving to ARTEMIS since we're not tracking individual SCMs assigned to each Facility like we might for an SW Asset.

EXISTING SCMs: This screen displays a grid of all SCMs that have been established for the site based on previous inspections and/or the site map. All listed SCMs must be inspected and photographed, and a compliance status must be entered for each. Do NOT delete any of the pre-populated SCM rows.
NEW SCMs: This screen should be used when a new SCM is observed that is not included in the list of existing SCMs, or if it is a new sites with no SCMs defined yet.

Site Activities/Areas: This screen displays a grid of all possible site activities/areas that may exist based on the type of stormwater permit. All activities/areas must be given an inspection status. Do NOT delete any of the pre-populated rows.
SCMNameOther (Only on form for consistency with NEW form)

entry_values.text: Already logged as PENDING Action Item
Already logged as COMPLETED Action Item

Issue Description (Amend/replace default text as needed)

entry_values.text: Already logged as PENDING Action Item

Issue Description (Amend/replace default text as needed)

entry_values.text: Not present at time of inspection
Already logged as PENDING Action Item
Already logged as COMPLETED Action Item

entries.label: If you encounter more than one issue for this Activity/Area, add additional issues by adding records to the Activity/Area grid
Issue Description (Amend/replace default text as needed)
Are Hostlers being fueled, maintained, or washed on site?
Hostler Maintenance Area Photo (if activities are not occuring during the inspection)
Are DSPs being fueled, maintained, or washed on site?
DSP Area Photo (if activities are not occuring during the inspection)
DSP Fueling / Maintenance Area Photo (if activities are not occuring during the inspection)
Is there Salt Storage on site?
Temporary Equipment on site (Add planned removal date if known)
Any wastewater discharge including exterior mop sinks, sand traps, oil-water separators, and/or grease traps
Are any of the following materials or activities exposed to precipitation, now or in the foreseeable future?

entry_values.text: Using, storing, or cleaning industrial machinery or equipment, and areas where residuals from using, storing, or cleaning industrial machinery or equipment remain and are exposed to stormwater.
Materials or residuals on the ground or in stormwater inlets from spills/leaks
Materials or products from past industrial activity
Material handling equipment (except adequately maintained vehicles).
Materials or products during loading/unloading or transporting activities.
Materials or products stored outdoors (except final products intended for outside use [e.g., new cars] where exposure to stormwater does not result in the discharge of pollutants).
Materials contained in open, deteriorated or leaking storage drums, barrels, tanks, and similar containers
Materials or products handled/stored on roads or railways owned or maintained by the discharger
Waste material (except waste in covered, non-leaking containers [e.g., dumpsters]).
Application or disposal of process wastewater (unless otherwise permitted).
Particulate matter or visible deposits of residuals from roof stacks and/or vents not otherwise regulated (i.e., under an air quality control permit) and evident in the stormwater outflow.

entries.label: Did you remember to take photos of the following things:
Control Measures: Did you log "Spill Protection Equipment"
Control Measures: Did you log "Stormwater Inlet and Outlet Structure"
Loading / unloading dock or shipping / receiving areas
Paved Rear & Sides of Building (not employee parking)
Hostler Parking / fueling area (Note distance between storm drain and Hostler Parking)
Any wastewater discharge including exterior mop sinks
Sand trap / oil-water separator/grease trap (if any)
Electric Van Charging Stations (Rivian Project Sites)
Any "Temporary" equipment (Lights, Generators, HVAC, etc.).  Note planned removal date, if known
DSP Parking Area (if onsite) - DSP are the vans and box cars used to deliver to consumers
If you have additional photos of certain Photo Type, add additional Photos by adding records to the Required Photos grid
Use this space for any additional notes or observations from the inspection:
Does the site map need to be updated to reflect current site conditions?
Describe site map updates that are needed
Attach photo of marked-up map reflecting the required changes

“I certify under penalty of law that this document and all attachments were prepared under my direction or supervision in accordance with a system designed to assure that qualified personnel properly gathered and evaluated the information submitted. Based on my inquiry of the person or persons who manage the system, or those persons directly responsible for gathering the information, the information submitted is, to the best of my knowledge and belief, true, accurate, and complete. I am aware that there are significant penalties for submitting false information, including the possibility of fine and imprisonment for knowing violations.”
